user.title: Add Roomba+
user.description: Select a discovered Roomba or add one manually.

manual.title: Manual setup
manual.description: Enter the IP address of your Roomba. You can find credentials at [{auth_help_url}]({auth_help_url}).

data_description.host: Local IP address of your Roomba

link.title: Link robot
link.description: Press and hold the **HOME** button on your {name} until it plays a series of tones (about 2 seconds), then release. The Wi-Fi LED will flash.

link_manual.title: Enter password
link_manual.description: Automatic password retrieval failed. Enter your Roomba password manually. Instructions: [{auth_help_url}]({auth_help_url}).

cloud_credentials.title: iRobot cloud credentials (optional)
cloud_credentials.description:
All commands (start, stop, room selection) run locally over MQTT and never touch the cloud. Cloud credentials are only used to read room names, mission history, and favorites — the robot is always controlled directly.

Enter your iRobot app email and password to enable these cloud features, or leave both fields empty to use local MQTT only.

data_description.irobot_username: The email address used to log into the iRobot app.

data_description.host: The local IP address of the Roomba.
data_description.password: The robot password (hold HOME button to retrieve).

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the robot. Verify it is powered on and reachable.
error.invalid_auth: Invalid credentials. Check your BLID and password.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.
error.cloud_auth_failed: Cloud authentication failed. Check your iRobot email and password.
error.cloud_unavailable: Could not reach iRobot cloud. Check your internet connection and try again.
error.invalid_cloud_credentials: Cloud authentication failed. Check your iRobot email and password.

abort.not_irobot_device: The discovered device is not an iRobot device.
abort.short_blid: A configuration flow for this robot is already in progress.
abort.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the robot at the specified address.

zones.title: Name detected rooms
zones.description: {zone_count} new area(s) detected. Assign names so you can clean them individually:

smart_zones.title: Name Smart Map zones
smart_zones.description: {zone_count} zone(s) have no name yet (IDs: {zone_ids}). Assign names so you can clean them individually:

data_description.continuous: Keep a persistent MQTT connection to the robot. Recommended.
data_description.delay: Seconds to wait between reconnection attempts.
data_description.map_enabled: Enable the live cleaning map for robots with position sensing (Roomba 900, i, s, j, Braava m).
data_description.map_size_px: Map canvas size in pixels (400–1200). Default: 600.
data_description.map_scale_mm_per_px: Millimetres per pixel (5–30). Default 10 = 6 m × 6 m at 600 px.

smart_zones_manual.title: Manually enter Smart Map zones
smart_zones_manual.description: Enter your room region IDs as a comma-separated list (e.g. 5, 12, 7). Find them in the HA diagnostics download under options → discovered_zone_ids, or start a room-specific clean from the iRobot app while HA is connected and check the diagnostics afterwards.

error.no_valid_ids: No valid entries found. Use the format id=Name, one per line.
error.pmap_not_resolved: Could not resolve the map ID. Ensure the robot is docked and connected, then try again.

cloud_credentials.title: iRobot cloud credentials
cloud_credentials.description:
Currently signed in as: {current_user}

Update your iRobot credentials or clear both fields to disable cloud features.

blocking_sensors.title: Blocking sensors
blocking_sensors.description: Configure sensors that must be inactive before a smart_start can begin.

data_description.blocking_sensors: Binary sensors that block a start when ON (e.g. door open, motion detected).
data_description.blocking_behavior: Abort immediately or queue the start until sensors clear.
data_description.blocking_timeout_min: How long to wait for sensors to clear before giving up.

map_management.title: Zone management
map_management.description:
Select a zone to rename or hide. Submit with no zone selected to save changes.

{zone_summary}

map_management_edit.title: Edit zone: {zone_name}
map_management_edit.description: Set a display name and visibility for this zone.

data_description.display_name: Custom name shown in selectors. Leave blank to use the default name.
data_description.hidden: Hidden zones are removed from selectors and will not trigger naming repair issues.

presence_scheduling.title: Presence-aware scheduling
presence_scheduling.description: Automatically unfreeze the cleaning schedule when everyone leaves home.

demand_cleaning.title: Demand cleaning
demand_cleaning.description: Automatically start a clean when the floor is significantly dirtier than usual.

rest980_migrate.title: Import rooms from roomba_rest980
rest980_migrate.description: Found {room_count} room(s) in your roomba_rest980 installation that aren't named yet in Roomba+: {room_names}. Import them now? Rooms you've already named here are left untouched.

error.cloud_auth_failed: Cloud authentication failed. Check your iRobot email and password.
error.cloud_unavailable: Could not reach iRobot cloud. Check your internet connection and try again.
error.invalid_cloud_credentials: Cloud authentication failed. Check your iRobot email and password.
error.cannot_connect: Could not connect. Check your internet connection and try again.

abort.integration_not_loaded: The integration could not be loaded. Check the logs and reload before opening settings.
abort.demand_cleaning_not_supported: Demand cleaning requires a SMART robot with cloud credentials configured.
abort.no_rest980_rooms_found: No rooms found in your roomba_rest980 installation.
abort.rest980_rooms_already_imported: All rooms from roomba_rest980 are already named in Roomba+.

zones_need_naming.title: New rooms detected
zones_need_naming.description: Roomba+ has detected new areas. Open the settings to name them.

smart_zones_need_naming.title: New Smart Map zones discovered
smart_zones_need_naming.description: Roomba+ discovered {zone_count} new zone(s) ({zone_ids}) with no name yet. Open the settings to name them.

init.title: Name Smart Map zones
init.description: Fill in a name for each zone below — each pre-filled line shows a zone ID, type the name after the equals sign. Zone IDs to name: {zone_ids}

data_description.zones: One entry per line: 21=Corridor, 22=Kitchen, etc. Comma-separated on one line is also accepted: 21=Corridor,22=Kitchen.

error.no_valid_entries: No valid entries found. Each entry must be in the format id=Name — one per line or comma-separated.
error.pmap_not_resolved: Could not resolve the map ID. Ensure the robot is docked and connected, then try again.

geometry_drifted.title: Map drift detected
geometry_drifted.description: The Roomba’s position map has drifted significantly. Re-confirm your room layout to keep the map accurate.

maintenance_baselines_reset.title: Robot runtime counter may have reset
maintenance_baselines_reset.description: The robot's bbrun.hr runtime counter is lower than the stored reset baselines for: {parts}. This usually means a firmware update silently reset the counter. Remaining-hours estimates for consumables are now unreliable. Reset all consumables using the Reset buttons.

performance_degradation.title: Cleaning performance declining
performance_degradation.description: Roomba+ has detected a sustained decline in cleaning speed over the last several missions (cause: {cause}). This may indicate worn brushes, a clogged filter, or significant changes in floor conditions. Check the device page for consumable status.

battery_recharge_high.title: High mid-mission recharging with degraded battery
battery_recharge_high.description: Your robot is spending a significant proportion of cleaning time recharging mid-mission while battery capacity has dropped below 75% of its original value. Consider replacing the battery.

battery_contact_suspect.title: Possible battery or dock contact problem
battery_contact_suspect.description: Roomba+ detected evidence of a battery/dock contact or communication issue (cause: {cause}). Recent charge-cycle peak battery levels: {recent_peaks}. A sudden implausible jump in reported battery level, or a declining trend in how high it gets each charge, usually points to a loose or corroded contact rather than the battery itself — clean the contacts on the robot and dock before assuming the battery has failed.

mixed_schedule.title: Competing schedule sources detected
mixed_schedule.description: In the last 30 days, {schedule_pct}% of cleans were started by HA schedules and {app_pct}% by the iRobot app. Competing schedules can cause double-cleans and missed rooms. Manage scheduling from one place only.

accident_detected.title: Possible floor accident detected
accident_detected.description: The last mission recorded an unusually high dirt event count in a very short runtime — a possible liquid spill or accident. Check the area before sending the robot back.

consecutive_skips.title: Robot skipped {count} consecutive cleans
consecutive_skips.description: Blocking sensors or presence scheduling prevented {count} consecutive scheduled cleaning opportunities. Check your blocking sensor configuration or presence schedule — they may be too restrictive.

map_drift_detected.title: Smart Map drift detected
map_drift_detected.description: The robot's recent missions show elevated drift from its expected dock position (latest: {magnitude_cm} cm, bearing {bearing}°). This may indicate the dock was moved or the robot is having difficulty relocating. Re-dock and allow a fresh mapping run. This notice clears automatically once drift returns to normal over the next few missions.

reloc_rate_elevated.title: Elevated relocalisation rate
reloc_rate_elevated.description: The robot has been relocalising significantly more often than its own established baseline ({baseline} per mission on average). This can indicate Smart Map degradation — consider deleting and re-training the map if you also notice "New space found" messages or inaccurate room tracking. This notice clears automatically once the rate returns to normal over the next few missions.

observed_zones_detected.title: Robot-detected obstacles in cloud map
observed_zones_detected.description: Your iRobot cloud map contains {zone_count} robot-detected obstacle zone(s). Run a few cleaning missions so Roomba+ can confirm these locally and build a more accurate obstacle map.

error_recurrence.title: Recurring error: {label}
error_recurrence.description: Error {error_code} ({label}) has occurred {count} time(s) in the last 30 days. Most recent: phase '{phase}', location '{room}'. Recommended action: {action}

cancellation_recurrence.title: Recurring cancellations
cancellation_recurrence.description: Missions have been cancelled {count} time(s) in the last 30 days ({by_user_count} by user/app, {other_count} other). Consider checking for schedule conflicts, app-side interventions, or whether the robot is being manually stopped.

schedule_suboptimal.title: Schedule may be missing high-dirt days
schedule_suboptimal.description: On {days}, dirt density was more than {threshold}× above the 30-day average and no clean was recorded. Consider adding a scheduled clean on those weekdays.

mission_anomaly.title: Unusual cleaning patterns detected
mission_anomaly.description: The last {count} missions showed abnormal patterns — the robot may be spending more time than usual covering less area, experiencing unusually long recharges, or encountering extreme dirt levels. Check for obstacles, worn brushes, or battery degradation.

stuck_pattern.title: Stuck pattern detected
stuck_pattern.description: Your Roomba gets stuck near {room} most often on {time}. Consider clearing the area before those cleans or adjusting your cleaning schedule.

smberr_high.title: High battery SMBus errors detected
smberr_high.description:
The robot has logged {count} SMBus communication errors between the main board and the battery chip. This often indicates worn dock contacts or an aging battery.

**Recommended actions:**
1. Clean the charging contacts on the robot and dock with a dry cloth.
2. If errors remain high after cleaning, the battery may need replacement.

dock_contact_health.title: Dock contact health issue detected

cloud_stale.title: Cloud data is stale
cloud_stale.description: Roomba+ has not received a successful cloud update for {minutes} minutes. Check your iRobot account credentials and internet connection. Local MQTT data is still available.

mqtt_watchdog.title: Robot MQTT connection lost during mission
mqtt_watchdog.description: Roomba+ has not received any MQTT messages from the robot for {minutes} minutes while a cleaning mission was active. Last known phase before going silent: {last_phase}. Robot cloud connectivity: {cloud_hint}. If the robot appears to be physically stuck or paused, this may not be a real network problem -- check the robot in person first.

integration_health.title: Integration health is degraded
integration_health.description: Roomba+'s integration health score has been below 50 for at least 30 minutes (current score: {score}/100). This combines active Repair Issue count, MQTT message age, and mission archive freshness. Check the sensor.*_integration_health entity's attributes for a breakdown of which signal is affected, and review any other active Repair Issues for this robot.

map_retrain_in_progress.title: Smart Map update taking longer than usual
map_retrain_in_progress.description: The robot's Smart Map has been updating for at least {minutes} minutes. A normal update is usually faster — this can happen after a large layout change. No action is needed yet; this will clear automatically once the update finishes.

map_retrain_stuck.title: Smart Map update appears stuck
map_retrain_stuck.description: The robot's Smart Map has been updating for at least {minutes} minutes without finishing. This is longer than a genuine update should take. Try restarting the robot from the iRobot app, or contact iRobot support if it persists.

maintenance_due.title: Maintenance is overdue
maintenance_due.description: The following consumable(s) have been due for replacement for at least 3 days: {items}. Check the maintenance_due binary sensor's attributes for exact overdue hours, then use the matching reset button or roomba_plus.reset_* action once replaced.

mqtt_watchdog_cloud_connected.title: Robot MQTT connection lost during mission
mqtt_watchdog_cloud_connected.description: Roomba+ has not received any MQTT messages from the robot for {minutes} minutes while a cleaning mission was active. Last known phase before going silent: {last_phase}. Robot cloud connectivity: connected — this points to a local/Home Assistant-side problem, not a Wi-Fi outage at the robot itself. If the robot appears to be physically stuck or paused, this may not be a real network problem -- check the robot in person first.

mqtt_watchdog_cloud_disconnected.title: Robot MQTT connection lost during mission
mqtt_watchdog_cloud_disconnected.description: Roomba+ has not received any MQTT messages from the robot for {minutes} minutes while a cleaning mission was active. Last known phase before going silent: {last_phase}. Robot cloud connectivity: disconnected — this suggests a Wi-Fi outage at the robot itself. If the robot appears to be physically stuck or paused, this may not be a real network problem -- check the robot in person first.

mqtt_watchdog_cloud_unknown.title: Robot MQTT connection lost during mission
mqtt_watchdog_cloud_unknown.description: Roomba+ has not received any MQTT messages from the robot for {minutes} minutes while a cleaning mission was active. Last known phase before going silent: {last_phase}. Robot cloud connectivity: unknown (this field isn't available on this firmware). If the robot appears to be physically stuck or paused, this may not be a real network problem -- check the robot in person first.

clean_room.name: Clean room
clean_room.description: Start a targeted clean of one or more named rooms on smart-map robots.

room_name.name: Room name
room_name.description: Room name or list of room names to clean. Must match the labels you assigned in Roomba+ options. Run a room-targeted clean via the iRobot app first if no rooms appear yet.

ordered.name: Ordered sequence
ordered.description: Clean rooms in the order supplied rather than letting the robot choose.

reset_filter.name: Reset filter
reset_filter.description: Record the current time as the filter replacement date.

reset_brush.name: Reset brushes
reset_brush.description: Record the current time as the brush replacement date.

reset_battery.name: Reset battery
reset_battery.description: Record the current time as the battery replacement date.

reset_pad.name: Reset cleaning pad
reset_pad.description: Record the current time as the pad replacement date (Braava only).

smart_start.name: Smart start
smart_start.description: Start a clean with blocking-sensor checks.

clean_sequence.name: Start sequence clean
clean_sequence.description: Start robot B when robot A completes its current mission. The listener fires once and removes itself automatically.

entity_id.name: Trigger robot
entity_id.description: Vacuum entity for robot A (must finish first).

target_entity_id.name: Target robot
target_entity_id.description: Vacuum entity for robot B (started after A finishes).

require_completed.name: Require completion
require_completed.description: When true, robot B starts only if robot A docked normally.

delay_minutes.name: Delay (minutes)
delay_minutes.description: Wait this many minutes after robot A docks before starting robot B.

rooms_not_found.message: Unknown room(s): {names}. Check the names match those assigned in Roomba+ options.

rooms_different_floors.message: All rooms must be on the same floor. Got rooms from different maps: {pmap_ids}.

not_smart_map.message: Room cleaning requires a Smart Map robot (i7, s9, j-series). This robot does not support Smart Maps.

no_rooms_configured.message: No rooms configured yet. To use this action: (1) Open the iRobot app, (2) Start a room-targeted clean for each room, (3) Return to Home Assistant — the rooms will appear automatically in the Roomba+ options where you can assign names.

pmap_not_resolved.message: Could not resolve map ID for one or more rooms. Ensure the robot has reported its map state via MQTT.

map_updating.message: The robot is currently updating its Smart Map. Wait for the readiness sensor to show 'Ready', then try again.

entity_not_found.message: Entity {entity_id} not found in the entity registry.

maintenance_store_unavailable.message: Maintenance store is not available for {entity_id}. Reload the integration and try again.

no_valid_segments.message: No segments matched the current map. The map may have been retrained — re-run a room mission in the iRobot app to refresh the room layout.

config_entry_not_found.message: No config entry found for {entity_id}. Try reloading the integration.

room_name_and_room_passes_conflict.message: Provide either room_name or room_passes, not both.

no_rooms_resolved.message: No rooms to clean — provide at least one room name.
